info.title: Direct Debit v2 API
info.description:
# Introduction

## Overview

The direct debit v2 API from IT Consortium (ITC) allows third parties to
debit customers across all supported mobile wallets, cards and bank
accounts specified in this document.

## Merchant Onboarding

The direct debit v2 provides three categories for third parties. They
are:

- **Subscriptions Only:** These are merchants whose integration is
  autodebits only.

- **Hybrid:** These are merchants whose integrations are autodebits
  and manual debits for failed autodebits.

- **Preauthorized debit only:** These are merchants whose integration
  is for manual preauthorized debits.

### Getting Started

To be given access to successfully integrate into this API, a client
needs to submit all required documents to ITC for onboarding. After
verification of all required documents, the following credentials are
issued to the merchant, each for specific purposes. Below are some
relevant credentials and their respective uses in direct debit API
integration and usage.

### Authentication 

All requests to direct debit require authentication. The main
credentials used for authentication are:

a. Transflow Id

b. Merchant Product Id

The aforementioned credentials will be given to the merchant after
successful onboarding.

### Direct Debit Merchant Catalogue Integration

As part of new features being offered to direct debit merchants, key
configurations are required as part of the onboarding process. These
credential set up will require a merchant to use their merchant
productId to retrieve configurations needed per product.

Each product of a merchant is expected to have key configurations as
part of the enhanced or advanced direct debit v2 features. Below are the
configurations and their significance:

| Configuration Name | Configuration Value | Interpretation |
|-------------------|---------------------|----------------|
| retryAttempts | Integer(minimum value 1) | Number of automatic retries permitted for a product. Any retry attempt beyond 4 has cost implications. |
| skipFactor | Integer(minimum value 1) | This works like a multiplier. Assuming a daily debit with a skip factor of 2, debit will take place every 2 days, if it's a weekly debit, it means debit will occur every 2 weeks. |
| daysToDebitDayNotice | string list | This refers to which day to the day of debit, a subscriber should be notified. It can be a list of days. Assuming a string list of 1, 3, the implication will be that the subscriber will be notified of a debit, 3 days and also 1 day from the day of debit. |


**Note**

1. In any situation where a payload which is expected to have any of
   these values aren't provided, the product's default configuration is used.

## Callbacks

The Direct Debit v2 API sends callback notifications to inform merchants about the status of various operations. Merchants must configure a callback URL during onboarding to receive these notifications.


### Debit Callback

This callback is sent after a debit transaction has been processed, whether it's a scheduled subscription debit, a triggered debit, or a one-time debit.

**Payload Structure:**
```json
{
  "responseCode": "01",
  "responseMessage": "Transaction processed successfully",
  "debitOrderTransactionId": "TXN123456789",
  "networkTransactionId": "NTX987654321",
  "merchantId": "MCHT001122",
  "productId": "PROD005",
  "mandateId": "MAND123459",
  "debitAccount": "233557207736",
  "debitAmount": "33.00",
  "reference": "REF2025123456",
  "narration": "INITIAL DEBIT CALL FOR ID 1765195913561.Y",
  "timestamp": "2025-12-08 12:12:58",
  "channel": "MTN",
  "charge": "0.33"
}
```

### Preapproval Callback

This callback is sent when a mandate (subscription, hybrid or preauthorization) has been successfully created or scheduled.

**Payload Structure:**
```json
{
  "responseCode": "01",
  "responseMessage": "Recurring subscription has been scheduled successfully",
  "mandateId": "MAND123459",
  "merchantId": "MCHT001122",
  "productId": "PROD005",
  "debitAccount": "233557207736",
  "reference": "REF2025123456",
  "channel": "MTN",
  "country": "GH"
}
```

### Response Codes

Common response codes you may receive in callbacks:

| Response Code | Description |
|--------------|-------------|
| 01 | Success - Transaction/Mandate processed successfully |
| 03 | Processing - Request is being processed |
| 100 | General Failure |
| 04 | Pre-approval pending. This callback responses are sent when a preapproval request remains uattended to by a merchant's customer, three minutes after it was sent. *If request remains unapproved after six minutes, a general failure callback (code 100) is sent.* |
| 111 | Inconclusive - Transaction inconclusive |
| 104 | Duplicate Transaction - A transaction with the same reference already processed|
| 101 | Insufficient Funds - Insufficient funds in customer account |
| 131 | Request Timeout - Request timed out |


### Callback Security

- All callbacks are sent via HTTP POST to your configured callback URL

responseMessage.example: subscription with this reference number already exists

debitAccount.description: Account from which debits are made

frequencyType.description: How often debit should be made

debitDay.description: Day within a week, month or year on which debit will take place

debitTime.description: Time of debit on debit day in HH:MM format (24-hour)

responseCode.description:
Response code indicating transaction status:
- 01: Successfully processed
- 03: Processing

value.description: Configuration value. For multiple values, use comma-separated format (e.g., "1,2,3")

debitAccount.description: Account from which debits are made

frequencyType.description: How often debit should be made

debitDay.description:
Day on which debit will take place.
- Daily: 1
- Weekly: 1-7
- Monthly: 1-28
- Yearly: 1-28

debitTime.description: Time of debit in HH:MM format (24-hour)

debitNotificationAccount.description: Account to receive prior debit notification. This is only optional for telco channels as the debit account is assumed as the debit notification account when it's not provided. For bank and card channels, debit notification account must be provided.

notificationStatus.description: Option to notify account holder prior to a debit

debitAccount.description: Account from which debits are made

frequencyType.description: How often debit should be made

debitDay.description:
Day on which debit will take place.
- Daily: 1
- Weekly: 1-7
- Monthly: 1-28
- Yearly: 1-28

debitTime.description: Time of debit in HH:MM format (24-hour)

debitNotificationAccount.description: Account to receive prior debit notification. This is only optional for telco channels as the debit account is assumed as the debit notification account when it's not provided. For bank and card channels, debit notification account must be provided.

notificationStatus.description: Option to notify account holder prior to a debit

debitTime.description: Time of debit in HH:MM format (24-hour)

debitNotificationAccount.description: Account to receive prior debit notification. This is only optional for telco channels as the debit account is assumed as the debit notification account when it's not provided. For bank and card channels, debit notification account must be provided.

startDate.description: Date from which manual preauthorization debit will be allowed

endDate.description: Date on which preauthorization will expire

responseCode.description:
Response code indicating preauthorization status:
- 01: Active - Preauthorization is active
- 03: Processing - Preauthorization is still being processed
- 100: General Failure
- 101: Mandate not found
- 109: Unauthorized merchant

responseMessage.description: Human-readable status message corresponding to the response code

referenceId.description: Reference Id of the preauthorization mandate

startDate.description: Date from which preauthorization debit is allowed

created.description: Timestamp when the mandate was created

updated.description: Timestamp when the mandate was last updated

post.description: This is used to send a subscription request. Note that configurations not provided will be replaced by the product's default configuration.
Update existing subscription. Only changed fields need to be provided.
Retrieve all subscriptions for a customer on a specific product

Schedule a one-time debit to be executed in the future.
The date of debit must be at least 24 hours from the date of schedule request.

Trigger failed subscription debits.
This privilege is available for merchants whose externalTriggerDebit is true.
Retrieve subscription details using third party reference number
Retrieve transaction status using transaction reference, product id and mandate id
Place a preauthorization request to a consumer

Trigger preauthorized debits.
This API is available for mandate or preauthorized only merchants.
Hybrid or subscription only merchants are not permitted.
Check the status of a preauthorization using its reference and product ID
Retrieve full mandate details for a preauthorization using its reference Id and product Id
